description_long: This house features a sprawling, multi-sectioned layout with a combination of gabled and hipped rooflines in a dark slate color. The exterior walls are finished in a light, neutral tone, likely representing painted siding or stucco. Multiple window groupings are visible, arranged in both symmetrical and asymmetrical patterns, with some sections featuring prominent bay windows. The main entryway is accentuated by a small porch with steps leading up, and the overall structure suggests a blend of traditional and modern suburban architectural elements. The house appears to have two stories, with the upper level set back in some sections, creating visual interest and depth. The property sits on a rectangular base, indicating a defined lot or plot in an urban neighborhood.
description: A large, two-story suburban house with a dark gabled roof, light exterior walls, and a prominent entry porch. The structure features multiple window groupings and a visually varied roofline.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry porch, symmetrical window arrangement, and the full width of the house.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the house, the setback of the upper story, and a mix of window placements.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying additional windows, a secondary entry or patio area, and the extended roof sections.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the house's length, roof complexity, and more asymmetrical window groupings.
attribute_window_arrangement: mixed symmetrical and asymmetrical groupings with some bay windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entry with small porch, steps, and a covered overhang, featuring a prominent front door
